title: dryopteris affinis subsp. affinis
intro: Dryopteris affinis subsp. affinis, also known as the golden-scaled male fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris affinis fern, a type of evergreen fern native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of the plant are attributed to its ability to treat various conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a diuretic. The plant is typically harvested and prepared for medicinal use through infusions, teas, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs for enhanced efficacy.
